page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-144-lssd is a high-capacity virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 144 vCPUs mapped to 72 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 540 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. The hardware configuration includes a massive local storage array consisting of 24 local NVMe SSDs, totaling 9383 GB of high-speed local storage. No dedicated GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 156,273.13 and a Passmark CPU score of 134,831.3. The Intel Xeon processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) contribute to top-tier performance in extended instructions, cryptography, and software compilation. Memory subsystem performance is highly competitive, registering a cached read bandwidth of 11,520.24 GB/sec and an uncached read bandwidth of 534.35 GB/sec. For database operations, the server achieved a top-tier Passmark database score of 35,667.37 and processed 207.97 million Redis SET operations per second.

Given the high density of compute cores and the integrated 9.3 TB NVMe SSD storage array, this instance offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive, I/O-heavy workloads. It is highly optimized for large-scale database systems, high-throughput web serving, and heavy multi-threaded data processing tasks like software compilation and bzip3 decompression. Additionally, the presence of Intel AMX instructions enables efficient CPU-based LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing where it achieves top-tier token throughput across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds for smaller models remain in the average tier.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-144-lssd is a high-performance virtual server configured with 144 vCPUs, 540 GB of RAM, and 9383 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 24 drives. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 72 physical cores, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance. It features advanced hardware capabilities including Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions, which accelerate cryptography, vector calculations, and CPU-based machine learning tasks. Benchmark data shows exceptional throughput in database operations, Redis transactions, and multi-threaded decompression. Lacking a dedicated GPU, it relies on its massive core count and fast local storage to handle demanding workloads. This server is highly suited for enterprise databases, high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, and large-scale data processing pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-144-lssd features 144 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 540 GB RAM, and 9383 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-144-lssd is a 144-vCPU server with 540 GB RAM and 9.3 TB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput databases.
tagline: A high-core-density compute and storage powerhouse featuring integrated NVMe SSDs and advanced vector acceleration for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 144 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors with hyper-threading.
9383 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 24 bundled drives.
540 GB of system memory with top-tier cached and uncached read bandwidth.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration for cryptography and AI inference.
Top-tier performance in database operations, Redis transactions, and web serving.
Best suited for enterprise databases, heavy data processing, and software compilation.
